title: Represent decimal multiplication with grids and area models
description: Practice using models like area models and hundredth grids to represent multiplication with decimals.
topic_path: Represent decimal multiplication with grids and area models

assessment_items.question_content:
The entire figure is one whole.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which multiplication equation best represents the figure?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: $\purpleE{\text{area}}=\blueE{\text{width}} \times \maroonE{\text{height}}$

The entire figure represents a whole.

The shaded part has a width of $\blueE{0.3}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

The shaded part has a height of $\maroonE{0.7}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

The overlapping area is $\purpleE{21\text{ hundredths}}$.

[[☃ image 1]]
The equation $0.21=0.3 \times 0.7$ best represents the figure.

options.alt: A large square with 100 equal parts. There are overlapping shaded sections. One section is 3 parts wide and 10 parts high labeled 3 tenths. The other is 10 parts wide and 7 parts high labeled 7 tenths. The overlapping area is 3 parts wide and 7 parts high

assessment_items.question_content:
The entire figure is one whole.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which multiplication equation best represents the figure?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: $\purpleE{\text{area}}=\blueE{\text{width}} \times \maroonE{\text{height}}$

The entire figure represents a whole.

The shaded part has a width of $\blueE{0.6}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

The shaded part has a height of $\maroonE{0.8}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

The overlapping area is $\purpleE{48\text{ hundredths}}$.

[[☃ image 1]]
The equation $0.48=0.6 \times 0.8$ best represents the figure.

options.alt: A large square with 100 equal parts. There are overlapping shaded sections. One section is 6 parts wide and 10 parts high labeled 6 tenths. The other is 10 parts wide and 8 parts high labeled 8 tenths. The overlapping area is 6 parts wide and 8 parts high.

assessment_items.question_content:
The entire figure is one whole.

[[☃ image 2]]

**Which multiplication equation best represents the figure?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: $\purpleE{\text{area}}=\blueE{\text{width}} \times \maroonE{\text{height}}$

The shaded part has a height of $\maroonE{0.2}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

The entire figure represents a whole.

The shaded part has a width of $\blueE{0.5}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

The overlapping area is $\purpleE{10\text{ hundredths}}$.

[[☃ image 1]]
The equation $0.5\times 0.2 = 0.1$ best represents the figure.

options.alt: A large square with 100 equal parts. There are overlapping shaded sections. One section is 5 parts wide and 10 parts high labeled 5 tenths. The other is 10 parts wide and 2 parts high labeled 2 tenths. The overlapping area is 5 parts wide and 2 parts high.

assessment_items.question_content:
Each large square is one whole.

[[☃ image 2]]

**Which multiplication equation best represents the figure?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We can think of multiplication as combining equal groups.

$\purpleE{\text{number of groups}} \times \blueE{\text{group size}} = \goldE{\text{total}}$
There are $\purpleE{6}$ equal groups of $\blueE{32\text{ hundredths}}$.
The total fills $\goldE{192\text{ hundredths}}$, which is the same as $\goldE{1\text{ whole}}$ and $\goldE{92\text { hundredths}}$.
The equation $6 \times 0.32 = 1.92$ best represents the figure.

options.alt: 2 large squares, each split into 100 equal parts. They have 6 outlined sections with 32 shaded parts in each and labeled 32 hundredths. There are 8 unshaded parts.

assessment_items.question_content:
Each large square is one whole.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which multiplication equation best represents the figure?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We can think of multiplication as combining equal groups.

$\purpleE{\text{number of groups}} \times \blueE{\text{group size}} = \goldE{\text{total}}$
There are $\purpleE{4}$ equal groups of $\blueE{47\text{ hundredths}}$.
The total fills $\goldE{188\text{ hundredths}}$, which is the same as $\goldE{1\text{ whole}}$ and $\goldE{88\text { hundredths}}$.
The equation $0.47 \times 4 = 1.88$ best represents the figure.

options.alt: 2 large squares, each split into 100 equal parts. They have 4 outlined sections with 47 shaded parts in each and labeled 47hundredths. There are 12 unshaded parts.

assessment_items.question_content:
Each large square is one whole.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which multiplication equation best represents the figure?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We can think of multiplication as combining equal groups.

$\purpleE{\text{number of groups}} \times \blueE{\text{group size}} = \goldE{\text{total}}$
There are $\purpleE{3}$ equal groups of $\blueE{46\text{ hundredths}}$.
The total fills $\goldE{138\text{ hundredths}}$, which is the same as $\goldE{1\text{ whole}}$ and $\goldE{38\text { hundredths}}$.
The equation $1.38 = 3 \times 0.46$ best represents the figure.

options.alt: 2 large squares, each split into 100 equal parts. They have 3 outlined sections with 46 shaded parts in each labeled 46 hundredths. There are 62 unshaded parts.

assessment_items.question_content:
The figure is not to scale.

![area model split into columns and rows. The columns have widths of 4 and 0.7. The rows have heights of 1 and 0.5.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1628c157a25db9605f96af23d01f000985045e2e)

**What multiplication expression could the area model above represent?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: ${\text{area}}={\text{width}} \times {\text{height}}$
![same area model with the total width labeled 4.7 and the total height labeled 1.5](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b53a2dbeba49338fc3d98d2343d55ce846faa669)
The area model represents $1.5 \times 4.7$.

assessment_items.question_content:
The figure is not to scale.

![area model split into columns and rows. The columns have widths of 10 and 9. The rows have heights of 8 and 0.3.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/362d8ae92f621eab6fd18018ff0b7f0a532cdab1)

**What multiplication expression could the area model above represent?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: ${\text{area}}={\text{width}} \times {\text{height}}$
![same area model with the total width labeled 19 and the total height labeled 8.3](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b399e8f4d261ae31fc88b3599593d0068e7425dd)
The area model represents $8.3 \times 19$.

The area model represents $98 \times 6.02$.
